text: Void role only. While resolving conflicts at this province, each character counts its combined [conflict-military] and [conflict-political] skill.
name: Massing at Twilight

text: Keeper role only. Action: During a [conflict-military] conflict, if your opponent counts more current [conflict-military] skill than you, choose a Berserker character you control – ready that character and move it to the conflict.
name: Crisis Breaker

text: Reaction: After a [clan-crab] character leaves play during the conflict phase – gain 1 fate. (Unlimited.)
flavorText: "I find the best use of my mouth is to bargain with it."
name: Yasuki Taka

text: Pride. Action: Choose another character you control – move an honored status token from this character to the chosen character.
name: Origami Master

text: Action: While this character is participating in a conflict, discard the Imperial Favor. Choose an opponent's participating character with 1 or more attachments – bow that character.
flavorText: "A student must learn every falsehood before they can learn the truth."
name: Itinerant Philosopher

text: Pride. (After this character wins a conflict, honor it. After this character loses a conflict, dishonor it.)
flavorText: "This wall is as unassailable as the honor of the Lion."
name: Miwaku Kabe Guard

text:
While you have claimed or are contesting:
- the [element-air] ring, this character gets +2[conflict-political].
- the [element-earth] ring, this character gets +2[conflict-military].
- the [element-fire] ring, this character gains pride.
name: Henshin Disciple

text: Reaction: After the conflict phase begins, choose a ring – while that ring is contested this phase, non-Spell events cannot be played.
flavorText: There are strict rules regarding the behavior of guests. There have never been any guests.
name: Master of Gisei Toshi

text: Action: Select one – move this character into a conflict or move this character home from a conflict. (Limit twice per round.)
flavorText: "I will follow you, and they will follow me. Even into Jigoku itself."
name: Moto Juro

text: This character gets +1[conflict-military] and +1[conflict-political] for each other Imperial card you control.
flavorText: "You are not worthy of her, Toturi-sama."
name: Seppun Ishikawa

text: Action: During the dynasty phase, discard the Imperial Favor – gain 2 fate.
flavorText: "So long as there is the light of 'I want to make a deal,' even the most painful negotiations will eventually pay off." – Yasuki Taka, the Wily Trader
name: Fair Accord

text: Action: Dishonor this character – each [clan-crane] character you control cannot be dishonored until the end of the phase.
name: Young Harrier

text: Seeker role only. Action: Name a card type. Reveal the top 3 cards of your conflict deck (top 4 cards instead if you control a Duelist character) – add up to 2 revealed cards of that type to your hand and discard the rest.
name: Test of Skill

text: Seeker role only. While attached character is ready, opponents cannot remove or gain fate from rings.
flavorText: There is no sorrow greater than wisdom.
name: The Stone of Sorrows

text: Attached character gains the [clan-lion] clan symbol and the Commander trait.
flavorText:
In the name of His Excellency,
the Right Hand of the Emperor,
High General,
Champion of the Lion,andnDaimyō of the Akodo,Toturi, son of Daio,
son of Kozue, daughter of Yokutsune...
name: Seal of the Lion

text: Seeker role only. Action: If you control a Shugenja character – remove up to 5 fate from among characters an opponent controls.
flavorText: "By these fires be cleansed." – Isawa Masahiro
name: Consumed by Five Fires

text: While this character is participating in a conflict, each other character that is dishonored does not count its skill toward the resolution of this conflict.
flavorText: "Let them fear the law and be better for it."
name: Cunning Magistrate

text: Conflict Action: Choose a participating character - bow that character, move it home, dishonor it, and remove 1 fate from it. For this phase, it loses all printed abilities.
name: A Fate Worse Than Death

text: Action: During a conflict, discard the Imperial Favor – set the base strength of the attacked province to 1 until the end of the conflict. Errata from RRG v1.1
flavorText: "We will bring the war to the Lion." – Moto Juro
name: Ride Them Down

text: Reaction: After the conflict phase begins - for this phase, each player has only one opportunity to declare a conflict, and may only declare a single conflict.
flavorText: "Knowing your advantages does you no good if your enemy is able to keep you from employing them." – Tao of Shinsei
name: Waning Hostilities
